title: Add support for --no-new-privs
body:
This replaces Dan's original PR #218.  As of now, no changes other than a rebase were required.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think we can actually remove it from the DB altogether, given that it's in the spec
@mheon @rhatdan im almost dead sure this is a selinux error.  @rhatdan did you say you were expecting a new selinux package for this too?
@baude are you able to get this to fail locally? Or just in the testing?  Is there a way I can get onto a testing machine where this is happening.

The `FAH27` one has:

> "kernel": "4.14.18-300.fc27.x86_64",

While the `Fedora fedora/27/cloud Podman` one has:

> "kernel": "4.13.9-300.fc27.x86_64",

As opposed to AH, Fedora usually does not update cloud images after release. You'd have to update the kernel and reboot as a separate step before starting the tests. Which is tricky of course, since the code is running *on* the host. One way around this is to define a `cluster` instead:

```yaml
cluster:
    hosts:
        - name: node
          distro: fedora/27/cloud
          specs:
              ram: 8192
              cpus: 4
    container:
        image: registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27
```

So then your code will run in the container, so you can e.g. `ssh node dnf update -y kernel` and then `ssh node reboot || :` or something along those lines.

Follow-up: you can now use `fedora/27/cloud/pungi`, which is updated at least once a day and should have the latest kernel (see https://github.com/projectatomic/papr/pull/87). But **caveat emptor**, there's no guarantee these images are going to be as stable as `fedora/27/cloud`.
ill add in some conditional check to the test looking for 4.14 or newer

:pushpin: Commit d415c57 has been approved by `rhatdan`
